rationale: The citing paper is a natural-experiment/quasi-experimental field study, and the cited paper is also a quasi-experimental field evaluation, so a methodological linkage is plausible from the notes. However, the substantive domains differ sharply (online WOM/observational learning vs. customer satisfaction quality programs), and the only evidence here is a single DOI-level reference match with no in-text citation context. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop, but importance is not clearly established.
